headline: 2 contracts catalogued on Ethereum; proxy pattern with upgradeable implementation, Coinbase-controlled roles including admin, owner, master minter, pauser, blacklister, and oracle

findings.text: Etherscan block explorer search surfaced token proxy 0xbe9895146f7af43049ca1c1ae358b0541ea49704 with linked implementation 0x31724ca0c982a31fbb5c57f4217ab585271fc9a5
No dedicated deployed contracts documentation page found on protocol.website; deployment details sourced from Etherscan and GitHub
No audit links recorded in per-protocol context; audit references found in CoinMarketCap note that OpenZeppelin audited cbETH smart contract
GitHub repo identified at https://github.com/coinbase/wrapped-tokens-os; confirmed as official source via Etherscan link and search results showing open-source code committed October 28, 2022
Protocol.chains lists only Ethereum; single-chain protocol, no cross-chain enumeration required
No factory contracts or child enumeration applicable; cbETH is a single token contract with fixed oracle and admin roles
Two on-chain addresses catalogued: proxy (token) and implementation (logic); both have verified source code on Etherscan indicating stable, audited architecture
All addresses in admin_addresses[] traced to verified Etherscan contract pages with source code publication

rationale.verdict: Discovery identified 2 core contracts on Ethereum: the proxy token contract at 0xbe9895146f7af43049ca1c1ae358b0541ea49704 and its upgradeable implementation StakedTokenV1 at 0x31724ca0c982a31fbb5c57f4217ab585271fc9a5. Administrative controls are centralized in Coinbase (owner, admin, master minter, pauser, blacklister, oracle roles). Source code verified on Etherscan; official repository at github.com/coinbase/wrapped-tokens-os. No additional peripheral contracts, factories, or multi-chain deployments identified for this run.

evidence.shows: cbETH proxy token contract with verified source code (FiatTokenProxy), implementation address 0x31724ca0c982a31fbb5c57f4217ab585271fc9a5 (StakedTokenV1), created at block 14133720 on 2022-02-03
StakedTokenV1 implementation contract with verified source code showing inheritance from FiatTokenV2_1, oracle and exchangeRate storage slots, mint/burn/pause/blacklist/admin role functions
Official open-source repository for Coinbase wrapped tokens including cbETH, containing StakedTokenV1.sol, FiatTokenProxy.sol, and infrastructure contracts; initial commit October 28, 2022

unknowns: D2-offchain: No canonical deployed contracts page found; only Etherscan sourced
D3-offchain: Audit status unclear; per-protocol context lists no audit_links; CoinMarketCap references OpenZeppelin audit but no link provided

protocol_metadata.about: Coinbase Wrapped Staked ETH (cbETH) is a liquid staking token that represents staked Ethereum (ETH2) held in Coinbase custody. Users can wrap their staked ETH to receive cbETH, an ERC-20 token that can be traded, transferred, and used in DeFi applications while the underlying ETH remains locked in Coinbase's staking infrastructure. cbETH operates via an upgradeable proxy contract inheriting from Centre's battle-tested FiatToken architecture, with Coinbase controlling all administrative roles including owner, admin, master minter, pauser, blacklister, and exchange-rate oracle.
